body:
### Your current environment
Hunyuan image fails to run when quantization is set to FP8.
### Your code version
The commit id or version of vllm ```text 0.24.0 ```
The commit id or version of vllm-omni ```text 0.24.0rc2.dev0+ga560ed184.d20260630 ```
### 🐛 Describe the bug ### Before submitting a new issue... - [x] Make sure you already searched for relevant issues, and asked the chatbot living at the bottom right corner of the [documentation page](https://vllm-omni.readthedocs.io), which can answer lots of frequently asked questions.

comments.author: akshatvishu
comments.body:
Hi @AbelSara ; This is due to vllm PR https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/43167 

Before that PR, model loaders could call:

```python
self.quant_config.get_cache_scale(name)
```

After that PR, vLLM moved KV-cache scale remapping into `AutoWeightsLoader` via:

```python
self.quant_config.get_cache_scale_mapper()
```

So any `vllm-omni` loader that still directly calls `get_cache_scale(name)` can fail with `AttributeError` when `quant_config` is non-`None`.

Can we apply the same fix to `vllm_omni/model_executor/models/hunyuan_image3/hunyuan_image3.py` at  #4808 that you have done for the `hunyuan_image3_transformer.py` ? (same cleanup should work here too!) as it still calls `self.quant_config.get_cache_scale(name)` in `HunyuanModel.load_weights`, so quantized AR loads can still hit this.

comments.author: lishunyang12
comments.body:
Root cause: this is the removed-`get_cache_scale` API path tracked in #4809 (vLLM #43167 replaced `quant_config.get_cache_scale(name)` with `get_cache_scale_mapper()` in v0.23.0). The Hunyuan-image FP8 serving failure hits it in two loaders:

- DiT transformer (`diffusion/.../hunyuan_image3_transformer.py`) — fixed by **#4808**.
- AR loader (`model_executor/.../hunyuan_image3.py`) — fixed by **#4810**.

Landing #4808 + #4810 resolves the FP8 serving failure here. For the *mixed* ModelOpt FP8/NVFP4 checkpoints there are two extra fixes (scalar `ndim==0` expert-scale tensors and the `<img_ratio>` tokenizer guard) carried by **#4597**, which should be trimmed to just those once the two above land. This issue can be closed once #4808 + #4810 merge.

title: [Bug]: Hunyuan-image fp8 cannot serve
